title: Karachi-Style Chicken Biryani

subs.name: Sona Masoori rice
subs.notes: reduce parboiling to 4 minutes; texture is softer

ingredients.name: onion, thinly sliced and fried until deep golden
whole spices: cardamom, cinnamon sticks, cloves, bay leaves
visual_checks: combined: 4 cardamom, 1 cinnamon stick, 4 cloves, 2 bay leaves
ingredients.notes: combined: 4 cardamom, 1 cinnamon stick, 4 cloves, 2 bay leaves

subs.name: kewra essence — 2 drops in 2 tbsp water

ingredients.name: saffron soaked in 2 tbsp warm milk

subs.name: a few drops of orange food colour mixed in milk

steps.text: Marinate the chicken with curd, ginger-garlic paste, red chilli powder, turmeric, coriander powder, half the garam masala, lemon juice, and salt. Mix well, cover, and set aside for at least 30 minutes — longer if time allows.

Fry sliced onions in oil until dark golden-brown and crisp. Remove and drain on paper. In the same oil, add whole spices and let them sizzle for 30 seconds, then add the marinated chicken along with chopped tomatoes and green chillies.

Cook the chicken on high heat for 5 minutes, then medium heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil separates and the chicken masala is thick. The gravy should be quite dry — almost sticking to the pan. Set the chicken masala aside.

Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to a rolling boil. Add the soaked and drained basmati rice with the remaining whole spices. Parboil for exactly 6 minutes — the grains should be 70% cooked and still have a slight chalky centre. Drain immediately.

In a heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven, spread a layer of chicken masala on the base. Top with a layer of drained rice. Scatter half the fried onions, half the mint, half the coriander, and drizzle with ghee. Repeat the layers ending with rice on top.

Drizzle rose water and the saffron-milk over the top layer of rice. Seal the pot tightly with a lid wrapped in a damp cloth or foil, place over a flat griddle (tawa) on the lowest possible flame, and cook on dum for 25 minutes.
Remove from heat and let rest for 10 minutes without opening. Open the lid, gently mix from the bottom upwards using a wide spatula to incorporate the layers. Serve with raita and salad.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: basmati-rice → Sona Masoori rice (reduce parboiling to 4 minutes; texture is softer); saffron → a few drops of orange food colour mixed in milk (gives colour without flavour); rose-water → kewra essence — 2 drops in 2 tbsp water (more concentrated; use sparingly)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: This recipe works perfectly in any Indian kitchen. Use a heavy aluminium degchi or pressure cooker base (lid removed) for the dum stage, placed on a cast-iron tawa over the lowest flame. Kewra essence is available in Indian grocery stores as 'kewra water' (Natraj or Patanjali brand). Kashmiri red chilli powder gives the characteristic bright colour without additional heat.
